description_long: This is a round, deep bowl with a wide opening and a slightly flared, uneven rim. The surface is matte and textured, showing a dark, earthy brown coloration with subtle variations and some lighter patches, suggesting it is made from fired clay or ceramic. The bowl's body is bulbous, tapering gently towards the base, and the rim appears hand-formed, giving it a rustic, utilitarian look. There are no visible handles or decorative elements, but one side features a small, slightly raised area and a couple of rectangular labels, possibly for cataloging or identification. The interior is similarly colored and textured, with no visible glaze or shine. Overall, the bowl appears robust and functional, suitable for holding or serving items.
description: A rustic, dark brown ceramic bowl with a wide mouth, matte textured surface, and a slightly uneven rim. It has a utilitarian, hand-formed appearance with no visible decoration.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, open mouth and the slightly flared, uneven rim, with the bowl's rounded body clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle reveals subtle surface texture and some lighter patches, as well as the start of a label on the right side.
description_view_2: The side view highlights two rectangular labels attached to the bowl, likely for identification, and shows the bowl's full curvature.
description_view_3: This view displays a small, raised area on the lower body and the bowl's overall roundness, with no visible labels.
attribute_location: kitchen or outdoor communal cooking area
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, ingredients, or liquids

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
